steps.title: Extract the angle correspondence from the similarity statement
steps.content:
The statement “triangle $CAE$ is similar to triangle $CBD$” defines a correspondence between vertices:
  • 1st vertices: $C \leftrightarrow C$
  • 2nd vertices: $A \leftrightarrow B$
  • 3rd vertices: $E \leftrightarrow D$
This means the angles at corresponding vertices are equal. Since $A$ corresponds to $B$, the angle at vertex $A$ in triangle $CAE$ (which is $\angle CAE$) corresponds to the angle at vertex $B$ in triangle $CBD$ (which is $\angle CBD$).

steps.title: Apply the property that corresponding angles in similar triangles are equal
steps.content: In similar triangles, all corresponding angles are equal, regardless of the scale factor between sides. Since $\angle CBD = 57°$ and $\angle CAE$ corresponds to $\angle CBD$, we have $\angle CAE = 57°$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
